channel.title: VS Code on Scoutman
channel.description: Recent content in VS Code on Scoutman

item.title: ⚜️ Azure Runbook Workbench: April 2026 Update
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
Building tools is never really finished. You ship something, people use it, and reality hands you a list of things you did not anticipate. This update is that list, worked through.

Azure Runbook Workbench just got a meaningful round of improvements : less about adding features, more about making what is already there actually dependable.

item.title: ⚜️ Azure Runbook Workbench: A Better Way To Build Azure Automation Runbooks In VS Code
item.author: stationsolutions@gmail.com (rodrigo pinto)
item.description:
I have worked with Azure Automation since the early days, and one thing kept bothering me: the development experience never really felt right.

Too much of the process ended up split between the Azure portal, local scripts, manual testing, and deployment steps living somewhere else. It worked, but it rarely felt like a proper development workflow.
